reasoning: All four share a common backbone (acoustic easement, secret-keeping vault, negative-space media slot, witnessed collective silence), so the win goes to whoever escapes that backbone with the strongest fifth axis. D does this best: its "Anti-Spectrum" model (certified true silence as a machine-readable calibration reference, deepfake watermark, and provenance chokepoint) is the single most original mechanism in the set, opening a whole trust/authentication frontier no other slot touches, and D explicitly spans the widest conceptual range (physical/contractual/negative-space/machine-null/ritual) with crisp "Distinct because" framing. A is a close second: its "Phantom Answer" anti-consultant who invoices for catastrophes-that-didn't-happen is genuinely bold and well-argued via the insurance analogy, with the most vivid prose, though four of its five overlap heavily with the common backbone. B differentiates well with the ocean-noise carbon credit (non-human beneficiary) and the reputational "Forgetting Engine," but its craft is thinner and the easement/confession/pause trio is generic. C is solid and clearly written but the safest: its outliers (a metered municipal damping utility, an event-licensing witness) are plausible-but-tame, and its fifth axis adds the least new conceptual territory, so it lands last.
